prs.title: [ROCm] [WIP not ready]AITER fused allreduce+rmsnorm: merge PRs #37646 + #81 with reviewer fixes
prs.author: rbrugaro-amd
prs.body_head:
…iewer fixes

  Merge vllm-project/vllm#37646 (AITER fused allreduce + RMSNorm pass) and
  EmbeddedLLM/vllm#81 (hidden_dim=7168 support + threshold tuning), then
  address maintainer feedback:

  - Add AITER compile range splitting in _set_compile_ranges() so the backend generates separate compiled graphs for fusion-eligible vs non-eligible token counts, matching the existing flashinfer pattern.
  - Convert RocmAiterAllReduceFusionPass to VllmFusionPatternMatcherPass and rewrite AITER patterns as VllmPatternReplacement subclasses. Add extra_check support to VllmPatternReplacement/register().
  - Extract _compute_allreduce_max_token_num() shared helper used by both AllReduceFusionPass (flashinfer) and RocmAiterAllReduceFusionPass.
  - Simplify parallel_state.py aiter capture context per review suggestion.

prs.title: [Core] Add `VLLM_GPU_SYNC_CHECK` env var
prs.author: njhill
prs.body_head:
vLLM now uses asynchronous scheduling by default and in the majority of cases. Performance relies on the absence of any gpu<->cpu synchronizations on the main cuda stream, but such syncs can be opaque and it is easy for them to creep in accidentally.

This change adds a `VLLM_GPU_SYNC_CHECK` which enables `torch.cuda.set_sync_debug_mode` for the model forward pass and sampler, so that we can easily check for such syncs.

I'm trying first to enable it globally in the CI to flush out syncs that need to be fixed or where they are unavoidable and the check needs to be suppressed. Will then probably split the fixes into separate PR(s).

prs.title: [ROCm] Expanded sparse MLA support
prs.author: ekuznetsov139
prs.body_head:
This PR:
* Fixes a bug in cache_kernels.cu that resulted in incorrect results for block_stride not multiple of 16
* Adds block size 16 to supported sparse MLA block sizes on ROCm
* Fixes a few bugs in the Python code
* Adds optimized Triton kernels with support of arbitrary block size for two of the steps
* In case of rocm_fp8_paged_mqa_logits, routes to the Triton kernel by default, since AITER  is slower for block size 1 and it does not handle block size 16 correctly

I've verified that, after this PR, both GLM-5-FP8 and DeepSeek-V3.2 work correctly on ROCm (MI325  / gfx942) with both --block-size 1 and --block-size 16.

prs.title: [ROCm] Rename LLMM1 to vecMatMul, refactor, and fix RDNA4 correctness bugs
prs.author: wjabbour
prs.body_head:
## Summary

Renames `LLMM1`/`LLGemm1_kernel` → `vecMatMul`/`vecMatMul_kernel` across the codebase, extracts helper functions (`load_tile`, `dot_row`, `warp_reduce`), replaces magic numbers with named constants, and adds stage-level comments documenting the four-stage reduction.

Also fixes two silent correctness bugs on RDNA4 (WARP_SIZE=32) that produced wrong results while passing on CDNA CI hardware:

- **Row write bug**: `lane % 32 == 0` hardcoded as the half2 write guard; on WARP_SIZE=32 rows beyond the first pair were never written. Fixed to `lane % (2 * THREADS_PER_ROW_GROUP)`.
- **Cross-warp reduction bug**: reduction loop bounded by `THREADS_PER_ROW_GROUP` instead of `num_warps`, silently dropping warp contributions when K > 2048. Fixed with a strided loop.

Regression tests for both bugs are included, verified red on unfixed code and green on the fix. Tests always pass on CDNA (WARP_SIZE=64) CI hardware — the comments explain why.

## Test plan
- `pytest tests/kernels/quantization/test_rocm_skinny_gemms.py -v` on gfx1201 (RDNA4, WARP_SIZE=32)
- 76/76 vecMatMul tests passing; regression tests confirmed red/green on unfixed/fixed code
- Not duplicating any open PR (checked via `gh pr list --search "LLMM1 OR LLGemm1 OR vecMatMul"`)
- AI assistance used (Claude); all changed lines reviewed by submitter

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/claude-code)

prs.title: [Quantization] quantized kv cache loading for models like qwen
prs.author: xuebwang-amd
prs.body_head:
## Purpose This PR aims to fix quantized kv cache model loading issues using AMD-Quark. The fix was initially applied to the `gpt-oss` model and may be extended to other models in `vllm/model_executor/models`, as they share a nearly identical methodology. Here is the original error traceback w/o this PR fixing. ## Here is a check list of some models defined in `vllm/model_executor/models/` for the supportness of quantized kv cache loading. Models with `get_cache_scale` called is marked as ✅, while models without `get_cache_scale` called is marked as ⚠️. The ⚠️ symbol is preferred over ❌ since some models can intrinsically have non-quantized KV cache, for instance: - VL models - MLA models - _etc_ Partial list is: ⚠️baichuan ⚠️bloom ✅ dbrx ⚠️ deepseek ⚠️falcon ⚠️ gemma ✅ gemma2 ✅ gemma3 ✅ gpt_j ⚠️ gpt_neox ⚠️ **_gpt_oss_**: this PR initially aims for ✅ grok1 ✅ hunyuan_v1 ✅ llama ✅ llama4 ✅ mixtral ⚠️ mixtral ✅ phimoe ⚠️ qwen ✅ qwen2 ⚠️ qwen2_moe ⚠️ qwen3_moe ... ## Test Plan ## Test Result ---

prs.title: [ROCm][fusion] enable ROCm rms_norm pattern matching in qk_norm_rope fusion
prs.author: gbyu-amd
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
Current qk_norm_rope fusion can only match the vLLM custom cuda rms_norm. This pr enables AITER custom rms_norm pattern matching for ROCm backends. 

## Test Plan
```bash
#!/bin/bash

export SAFETENSORS_FAST_GPU=1
export VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER=1
export VLLM_RPC_TIMEOUT=1800000
export VLLM_LOGGING_LEVEL=DEBUG

vllm serve Qwen/Qwen3-0.6B \
    --tensor-parallel-size 1 \
    --max-num-batched-tokens 32768 \
    --trust-remote-code \
    --no-enable-prefix-caching \
    --gpu_memory_utilization 0.9 \
    --compilation-config '{"cudagraph_mode": "FULL_AND_PIECEWISE", "pass_config": {"enable_qk_norm_rope_fusion": 1, "enable_noop": true}, "custom_ops": ["+rms_norm", "+rotary_embedding"]}' \
    --async-scheduling
```

## Test Result
Below log can be observed from the server side:
```bash
(EngineCore_DP0 pid=349086)^[[0;0m DEBUG 11-23 13:46:08 [compilation/qk_norm_rope_fusion.py:235] Fused QK Norm+RoPE on 1 sites
```

Dataset acc with main:
```bash
|Tasks|Version|     Filter     |n-shot|  Metric   |   |Value |   |Stderr|
|-----|------:|----------------|-----:|-----------|---|-----:|---|-----:|
|gsm8k|      3|flexible-extract|     5|exact_match|↑  |0.4185|±  |0.0136|
|     |       |strict-match    |     5|exact_match|↑  |0.4193|±  |0.0136|
```

Dataset acc with this pr:
```bash
|Tasks|Version|     Filter     |n-shot|  Metric   |   |Value |   |Stderr|
|-----|------:|----------------|-----:|-----------|---|-----:|---|-----:|
|gsm8k|      3|flexible-extract|     5|exact_match|↑  |0.4139|±  |0.0136|
|     |       |strict-match    |     5|exact_match|↑  |0.4139|±  |0.0136|
```
